hero.eyebrow: Sustainable tourism for Ham Ninh fishing village
hero.title: Ham Ninh AI Guide — Preserve Heritage. Support Tourism. Fairness for Fishermen.
hero.description: A production-oriented AI travel guide combining grounded cultural answers, place search, clear evidence, and fair local recommendations for Ham Ninh fishing village, Phu Quoc.

trustBadges.label: Fair re-ranking
trustBadges.description: Adds local business signals to recommendation order.

trustBadges.label: Bilingual content
trustBadges.description: Vietnamese and English content share the same structure.

problem.heading: Tourism can grow quickly while local value is left behind
problem.body: When large platforms optimize for popularity, Ham Ninh’s cultural stories and small local businesses need a fairer introduction layer.

cards.title: Over-tourism & cultural erosion
cards.description: Fragmented information can reduce the village to photo spots without explaining its fishing heritage.

cards.title: Platform economic bias
cards.description: Businesses with more reviews or marketing budgets often outrank local family-run providers.

cards.title: Missing accurate local sources
cards.description: Cultural answers should come from retrieved documents and citations, not model guesswork.

solution.heading: A guided assistant connecting culture, places, and local context
solution.body: The product experience focuses on useful travel outcomes: grounded cultural answers, place discovery, transparent recommendation evidence, and recoverable agent progress.

pillars.title: RAG Agent — Local Guide
pillars.description: Answers culture, history, and food questions using retrieved sources.

pillars.title: Maps & Service Agent — Concierge
pillars.description: Planned to connect with Places API for places, routes, and accessibility context.

pillars.title: Fairness Re-ranker — Local Balance
pillars.description: Uses deterministic, auditable scoring signals to balance relevance, quality, proximity, accessibility, and local representation.

techStack.heading: Academic stack, designed for real operation
techStack.body: The components support i18n, knowledge retrieval, agent orchestration, RAG evaluation, and system observability.

items.name: Next.js 16
items.description: Frontend App Router, locale routes, and responsive landing UI.

items.name: FastAPI
items.description: API gateway for chat, SSE, and health checks.

items.name: LangGraph
items.description: Supervisor and worker agent orchestration with checkpoints.

items.name: Qdrant
items.description: Vector database for Ham Ninh cultural RAG.

items.name: Goong Maps Platform
items.description: Place and routing data source in later slices.

items.name: Deterministic fairness ranking
items.description: Auditable scoring and balancing for local, relevant, accessible place recommendations.

items.name: RAGAS
items.description: Evaluates faithfulness, relevance, and context recall.

items.name: Langfuse
items.description: Traces, latency, and cost for agent workflows.

demo.heading: The experience shell opens progressively by slice
demo.body: In this slice, the landing page describes target capabilities. Real chat, map, and architecture functionality will be connected in later slices.
demo.note: CTAs may lead to scaffold or in-progress pages; they do not imply fully working chat/map behavior before S03.

items.title: Citation-backed chat
items.description: Planned to show RAG answers with sources and a reasoning log.

items.title: Re-ranked map

items.title: Transparent architecture
items.description: Planned to explain the supervisor, agents, guardrails, and observability.

Map.mapUnavailable: Google Map could not initialize. Result cards and details remain available.

overview.heading: Multi-agent AI assistant for Ham Ninh fishing village
overview.body: The architecture combines Retrieval-Augmented Generation for cultural knowledge, place and route tooling, deterministic fairness re-ranking, and LangGraph-based agent orchestration — all served through a bilingual Next.js frontend with full observability.

cards.title: Data Flow
cards.description: User → Frontend → Agent Orchestrator → Workers (RAG, Maps) → SSE Streaming → Response

ragPipeline.heading: RAG Pipeline — Citation-backed cultural Q&A
ragPipeline.body: Answers about Ham Ninh's culture, history, and cuisine are grounded in retrieved documents — not model guesswork. Every answer includes citations and is evaluated by RAGAS for faithfulness and relevance.

cards.title: Document Corpus
cards.description: Cultural documents are normalized, chunked, and embedded into a Qdrant vector store for fast similarity search.

cards.title: Vector Search
cards.description: User queries are embedded and matched against the corpus using top-k similarity with configurable thresholds.

cards.title: Grounded Answers
cards.description: The LLM generates answers anchored in retrieved context, with inline citations back to source documents.

mapsApi.heading: Maps & Places API — Real-world place data
mapsApi.body: Google Places API (New) provides nearby search, text search, place details, and autocomplete. Routes API powers computeRouteMatrix for distance-aware recommendations. A circuit breaker ensures graceful fallback when the API is unavailable.

cards.title: Place Discovery
cards.description: Nearby search and text search find restaurants, services, and cultural sites near Ham Ninh fishing village.

cards.title: Route Planning
cards.description: computeRouteMatrix calculates distances between user location and candidate places for proximity-aware ranking.

cards.title: Fallback Handling
cards.description: Circuit breaker detects API failures and returns curated fallback results instead of error pages.

fairnessReranking.heading: Deterministic scoring for local, useful recommendations
fairnessReranking.body: Provider results are treated as candidates, then filtered and ranked with auditable signals: relevance, proximity, quality, geo-locality, popularity damping, accessibility, and provider health. The UI exposes receipts and explanations, not internal policy axes.

cards.title: Candidate suitability
cards.description: Places that do not match the user's travel intent are filtered before ranking instead of being shown as raw provider dumps.

cards.title: Auditable scoring
cards.description: The score breakdown explains practical signals such as relevance, distance, quality, locality, and accessibility.

cards.title: Fairness audit
cards.description: Each recommendation run records candidate counts, local representation, metadata gaps, provider status, and fallback warnings.

orchestration.heading: Agent Orchestration — LangGraph supervisor pattern
orchestration.body: A LangGraph supervisor coordinates specialized worker agents: the RAG Agent for cultural answers and the Maps & Service Agent for place data. Guardrails filter unsafe inputs, and SSE streaming delivers real-time chat responses with session durability.

cards.title: Supervisor Pattern
cards.description: The supervisor routes queries to the right worker agent based on intent — RAG for culture, Maps for places.

cards.title: Streaming Responses
cards.description: Server-Sent Events (SSE) deliver token-level streaming for a responsive chat experience.

cards.title: Session State
cards.description: LangGraph checkpoints preserve conversation history and agent state across session boundaries.

frontendShell.heading: Frontend Shell & Observability
frontendShell.body: Next.js 16 App Router powers the frontend with next-intl for bilingual support (English/Vietnamese). Langfuse traces agent workflows, health endpoints monitor system status, and responsive UI ensures accessibility across devices.

cards.title: App Shell
cards.description: Next.js 16 with App Router, responsive layout, and progressive enhancement from landing page to full chat/map.

cards.title: i18n Support
cards.description: next-intl provides locale routing, message files, and server-side translation for all UI text in English and Vietnamese.

cards.title: System Observability
cards.description: Langfuse traces, health endpoints, and structured logging provide visibility into agent performance and system health.
